text.ru:
Цветущий кристалл

Империи возникают и рушатся, пока алмаз растёт в утробе мира.

Жизнь — собор, Цветущий кристалл. Её красота складывается ледниково медленно. Горы поднимаются из неторопливого танца земных нитей. Сердца древних камней превращаются в самоцветы, всё более сложные и прекрасные под медленной капелью тысячелетий. Ослепительные и неуловимые, как куртуазные возлюбленные, они чаруют игрой отражающих граней. Озёра исчезают под землёй, в тайных пещерах со звёздными сводами. Леса поднимаются из пустых котловин, их пышные волосы волнуются в душном жаре.

Чудеса складываются из малых движений.

Цветущий кристалл, величие нельзя создать за один день — и не потому, что тебе не хватает способности. Красота кристаллизуется во времени: это большая временная структура, построенная из малых, но намеренных жестов. Немногие решаются на такие дела, пугаясь их масштаба. Но те, кто решается, двигают горы.

Я знал одного человека, который сдвинул гору. Это случилось много веков назад, в далёком уголке Китая. Старик по имени Юй-гун досадовал на расположение своего дома: прямо перед дверью стояли две огромные горы, и пути к деревне по другую сторону не было. Юй-гун позвал сыновей помогать ему понемногу раскапывать горы и уносить землю, чтобы открыть дорогу. Сосед, услышав этот план, рассмеялся: «Какая глупость! Как ты надеешься сдвинуть две горы?» Юй-гун ответил, что после его смерти работу продолжат сыновья, потом их сыновья, и так далее. Рано или поздно труд, начатый в тот день, будет завершён. Верный своим словам, он каждый день копал гору и бросал камни у моря. Поражённые его настойчивостью, боги велели перенести горы, и старик достиг невозможной цели ещё при жизни.

Цветущий кристалл, решимость и постоянство способны вызвать бога из машины. Удача приходит к тем, кто предан регулярному усилию: твоя усердность будет вознаграждена, возможно, неожиданным образом. Иди шаг за шагом. Твои цели велики, но требуют маленьких, точных движений. Большие замыслы — это храмы, возведённые твоему намерению. Ни прочный фундамент, ни изысканный интерьер нельзя построить быстро. Хотя успех, любовь и творчество иногда кажутся чудесами из воздуха, они возникают, когда мозаика рассчитанных действий сходится воедино. Чем щедрее ты относишься к своему времени и заботе, тем богаче будет результат.
text.en:
Blossoming Crystal

Empires rise and fall while a diamond grows in the womb of the world.

Life is a cathedral, Blossoming Crystal. Its beauty forms glacially. Mountains rise out of the slow dance of the earth’s filaments. The hearts of ancient rocks turn to jewels, ever more complicated and beautiful with the slow drip of millennia. As dazzling and elusive as courtly lovers, their enchantment radiates from the play of reflective surfaces. Lakes disappear underground, into secret caverns with star-strewn ceilings. Forests swell out of empty basins, their lush hair undulant in waves of cloying heat.

Miracles are the sum of small movements.

Blossoming Crystal, grandeur cannot be wrought in a day, and not because you lack the ability to do so. Beauty crystallizes over time, a grand temporal structure built by small, yet deliberate gestures. Few dare undertake such feats, inhibited by their enormity. Those who do, however, move mountains.

I once knew a man who moved a mountain. This happened centuries ago, in a far-flung corner of China. The old man, named Yugong, was exasperated by the placement of his home. His doorway faced the foot of two great mountains, with no path leading to the village on the other side. Yugong summoned his sons to help dig up the mountains bit by bit and dispose of them, thus clearing the path. His neighbor, overhearing the plan, laughed, and said “What a silly idea! How could you possibly hope to move two mountains?” To this, Yugong replied that after his death, his sons would continue this work, and their sons after them, and so on. Eventually, the travail beginning on that day would be complete. True to his words, Yugong kept digging away at the mountain every day, taking bits of rubble and tossing them by the sea. Awed by his concerted effort, the gods ordered that the mountains be moved, and the old man reached his impossible goal within his lifetime.

Blossoming Crystal, determination and consistency can summon god from the machine. Luck befalls those devoted to regular effort—you will be rewarded for your diligence, perhaps in surprising ways. Take your journey one step at a time. Your goals are vast, yet require small, elegant movements. Major aims are temples erected to your purpose. Neither a strong foundation nor an exquisite interior can be built quickly. Though success, love, and creation may seem like miracles materializing out of thin air, they are formed when a mosaic of calculated actions comes together. The more generous you are with your time and care, the more rewarding the result will be.

text.ru:
Танцующий в круге

Полотно радости соткано из родственных душ.

Тебе суждено найти своих людей, Танцующий в круге. Одинокий в толпе, ты спрашиваешь себя, не станет ли одиночество твоим спутником на всю жизнь. Люди сидят друг напротив друга и притворяются, что их параллельные монологи — это разговор. Вопросы, брошенные на ветер, не ждут ответа. Слова тщательно подбираются, чтобы заполнить пустую комнату, не переступая границу сердца. Отношения разворачиваются как заводные игрушки: тебя не покидает ощущение, что если заменить участников, ход событий останется тем же. Бесчисленные люди пожимают руки или обнимаются, но так и не касаются друг друга по-настоящему. Часто близость оказывается лишь номинальной. Танцующий в круге, не теряй надежды: ключ к правильному кругу — в том, кто ты есть.

Ищи людей, чьи души сделаны из того же материала, что и твоя.

Дружба похожа на магнетизм росинок. На травинках или в чашечках лепестков росинки мерцают одиноким светом. Ни растрёпанные папоротники, ни задумчивые ландыши не могут удержать эти бусины настолько, чтобы назвать их своими. Но встреча двух росинок преображает их с силой обречённой любви: граница между ними растворяется, и они становятся одним. Сходство обещает радость соединения, но открытие сходства требует жертвы. В отличие от росинок, люди непрозрачны. Чтобы найти родственные души, нужно обнажить свою — а это нелегко.

Однажды я видел торговца, который катил тележку со странными фонарями по ночному рынку. Это было много веков назад, когда человеческие дни были сотканы из волшебства и обыденности. Торговец утверждал, что продаёт лекарство от одиночества. Сами фонари стоили дёшево, но цена их использования была головокружительной. С помощью колдовства он извлекал сердце владельца фонаря и помещал его внутрь. Рядом с его багровым светом родственные сердца вспыхивали, как светляки летней ночью, позволяя владельцу найти подлинное товарищество.

Танцующий в круге, пусть тебя не останавливает жертва открытости. Рука об руку с теми, чьё сердце и разум близки твоим, двигайся по жизни так, будто она — танец.
text.en:
Circle Dancer

The cloth of joy is woven from kindred souls.

You are destined to find your people, Circle Dancer! Alone in a crowd, you wonder whether solitude will be your lifelong companion. People sitting across from each other pretend that their parallel soliloquies are a conversation. Questions tossed to the wind await no answer. Words are chosen carefully to fill an empty room without breaching the heart’s frontier. Relationships unfold like wind-up toys—you cannot shake the feeling that if their participants were replaced, the course of events would still remain the same. Countless people shake hands or embrace, yet never truly touch. Oftentimes, closeness feels nominal. Circle Dancer, do not give up hope—the key to finding the right company is who you are.

Seek people whose souls are made of the same material as yours.

Friendship resembles the magnetism of dewdrops. Perched on blades of grass or cupped gently by a petal, dewdrops shimmer with a lonesome light. Neither frayed ferns nor wistful lilies of the valley can keep these beads long enough to call them their own. Yet the meeting of two dewdrops transforms with the force of star-crossed love: the barrier between them liquefies and they become one. Likeness promises the delight of synthesis, but the discovery of likeness requires sacrifice. Unlike dewdrops, humans are opaque. To find twin souls, one must bare one’s own, which is no easy feat.

I remember once seeing a merchant pushing a cart filled with strange lanterns at the night market. This was centuries ago, when the days of people were woven from enchantment and mundanity. The merchant claimed that he was selling the cure for loneliness. The lanterns themselves were cheap, but the price for their usage was vertiginous. The merchant would help extract the lantern owner’s heart by way of sorcery and plant it within the lantern. In proximity to its crimson glow, kindred hearts would light up like fireflies on a summer night, allowing its owner to find true companionship.

Circle Dancer, do not let the sacrifice of opening up deter you. Hand-in-hand with those whose heart and mind are close to yours, move through life as though it were a dance.

oracleComment.ru: Танцующий в круге, пусть тебя не останавливает жертва открытости. Рука об руку с теми, чьё сердце и разум близки твоим, двигайся по жизни так, будто она — танец.
oracleComment.en: Circle Dancer, do not let the sacrifice of opening up deter you. Hand-in-hand with those whose heart and mind are close to yours, move through life as though it were a dance.

text.ru:
Даритель

Дающая рука наполняет сердце.

Есть лишь одно лекарство от первичной раны, Даритель. Я хорошо знаю эту грызущую пустоту, которая поднимается в тишине. Она питает вечное движение человечества. Лихорадочное изобретательство постоянно перестраивает мир; размытость его вечного становления приглушает рану. Но стоит войти в тихие воды бытия — она снова здесь, воет от ненасытного голода. Прекрасные предметы, любовные признания, невероятные подвиги исчезают в ней и обращаются в пепел. Отчаянно пытаясь наполнить эту бушующую печь, люди взбираются всё выше. Даритель, ими руководит тяжкая ошибка. Никакое приобретение их не спасёт.

Только отдавая другим, можно утолить голод души.

Когда боги создавали людей, они сделали их тела из земли и воды — твёрдости и гибкости. Душу они сотворили из воздуха и огня. Воздух оживил порыв дарить и создавать. Огонь породил страсти — похоть, голод, честолюбие. Он создал и смену эпох, и ненасытное желание в человеческом сердце. Не бросай жертвы в эту бездонную расщелину, надеясь её утолить. Открой сердце, пусть дары льются наружу, наполняя чужие чаши и твою собственную. Вселенная ответит на твою щедрость.

Я помню пожилую пару, которая брела по снежным склонам к деревне. Они сделали соломенные шляпы, чтобы продать их на рынке. Был канун Нового года, и у них не было ни монеты. После долгого дня на лютом холоде они не продали ни одной шляпы. Смирившись, они пошли обратно вверх по крутой тропе к своему горному дому. По дороге они увидели ряд статуй Дзидзо, беспощадно избиваемых ледяным ветром. Пожалев их, старики надели на статуи соломенные шляпы, отдав даже те, что носили сами. Измученные, муж и жена уснули, едва добравшись домой, но их разбудили громовые удары. Распахнув дверь, они увидели, как внутрь хлынули золотые монеты и вкусная еда. Вдалеке статуи Дзидзо спускались по горной тропе, всё ещё в соломенных шляпах.

Даритель, подумай, что ты можешь дать другим. Материальные дары и помощь прекрасны, но это не единственное, что можно предложить. Внимательное слушание, минута участия и добрые слова часто уже достаточны.
text.en:
Gift Giver

The giving hand fills the heart.

There is but one cure for the primordial wound, Gift Giver. I know it well—that gnawing emptiness which emerges in stillness. It fuels humanity’s perpetual motion. Feverish invention constantly reconfigures the world; the blur of its eternal becoming quietens the wound. Yet as soon as one enters the still waters of being, there it is again, howling with a ravenous hunger. Beautiful objects, amorous confessions, incredible feats vanish into it, and turn to ash. Desperate to fill this raging furnace, humans scale ever greater heights. Gift Giver, they are guided by grave error. No acquisition can save them.

Only giving to others will soothe the soul’s hunger.

When the gods crafted humans, they made their bodies from earth and water—firmness and flexibility. The soul they wrought from air and fire. Air animated the impulse to give and to create. Fire gave rise to passions—lust, hunger, ambition. It wrought both the progression of ages and an insatiable desire within the human heart. Do not toss sacrifices into this bottomless ravine in hopes to quench it. Open your heart, let gifts pour forth to fill others’ cups and your own. The universe will reciprocate your generosity.

I remember once seeing an elderly couple trudging down snowy slopes to a village. They had crafted straw hats to sell at the market. It was New Year’s Eve and they did not have a coin to their name. After a long day of standing in the biting cold, they hadn’t sold a single hat. Resigned, they plodded back up the steep path to their mountain dwelling. As they climbed, they noticed a row of Jizō statues, pelted mercilessly by the chilly wind. Taking pity on them, the old couple dressed them in the straw hats, giving up even those they had been wearing themselves. Exhausted, husband and wife fell asleep as soon as they reached home, yet both were awoken by thunderous thuds. As they flung the front door open, an avalanche of gold coins and delicious food poured inside. In the distance, they saw the Jizō statues walking back down the mountain path, still wearing the straw hats.

Gift Giver, think about what you can give to others. Material gifts and a helping hand are wonderful, but not the only things one can offer. Careful listening, a moment of attention, and kind words are often enough.

text.ru:
Неподвижный наблюдатель

Снег — это способ земли видеть сны.

Неподвижный наблюдатель, я приветствую тебя мягко. Ты идёшь по замёрзшему озеру, сопротивляясь колючему ветру. Лёд предательски потрескивает, под ним свивается чёрная вода. Берег и небо сливаются — костяно-белые и пустые. Вдалеке скелетные деревья поднимают пепельные руки к отсутствующему солнцу.

Наблюдатель, эта зима — сон: полусвет, полуправда. Её сонное прикосновение притупляет разум, но тревожит сердце. Предупреждаю: действовать сейчас опасно. Не гонись за мерцанием, которое дразнит тебя через лёд. Видишь ли ты впереди блеск сокровища или слышишь позади шёпот мятежа — остановись и скажи:

Я жду.

Я жду.

Твои карманы полны спящих семян. Не рассыпай их сейчас. Тот, кто сажает до оттепели, пожнёт ветер. Зима рисует мираж цветения на ледяном стекле, но морозные цветы не имеют запаха. Чтобы твои семена не погибли в снегу, так говорит мороз:

Наблюдатель, я не прошу тебя отказаться от поисков. Прими неподвижность, ощути простоту проторенных дорог. В этом благодать застоя: отдыхать, выдерживать и хранить семена. Когда земля повернётся во сне, весна ослабит её ледяное дыхание. Тогда ты узнаешь, где посадить свой сад. Неподвижность — сила, когда мир спит.
text.en:
Still Watcher

Snow is the earth’s way of dreaming.

Still Watcher, I greet you gently. You tread across the frozen lake, bracing against the biting wind. The ice crackles treacherously, black water coils below. Shore and sky coalesce, bone-white and barren. In the distance, skeletal trees lift their ashen arms toward an absent sun.

Watcher, this winter is a dream: half-light, half-truth. Its soporific touch dulls the mind yet stirs the heart. I warn you—acting now is perilous. Do not chase the flicker that taunts across the ice. Whether you glimpse the glitter of treasure ahead, or hear the murmurs of mutiny behind, halt and say:

I wait.

Your pockets are filled with sleeping seeds. Do not spill them now. He who plants before the thaw will harvest the wind. Winter crafts its mirage of bloom across the frozen glass, yet the frost paints flowers that bear no scent. Lest your seeds perish in the snow, thus speaks the frost:

Nature holds its breath; move now, and you will break its rhythm. Winter draws the world back into its bones. Time sleeps in its own stomach and dreams of the next world to exhale. Beneath the frozen soil, the forgotten queen turns the pomegranate in her hand, its seeds bitter. The earth hides her face, folds her colors, and lets her pulse sink deep into the dark. The wise retrace their own footprints. Trust the paths, the people, the principles that have never betrayed you.

Watcher, I do not ask you to forsake your quest. Welcome the stillness, savor the simplicity of well-trodden roads. This is the grace of stalemate: to rest, to endure, and to guard your seeds. When the earth turns in her sleep, spring will loosen her frozen breath. Then you will know where to plant your garden. For stillness is strength when the world’s aslumber.

Время спит в собственном желудке и видит сон о следующем мире, который выдохнет наружу. Под мёрзлой почвой забытая царица поворачивает в руке гранат, его зёрна горьки. Земля прячет лицо, складывает свои цвета и опускает пульс глубоко во тьму. Мудрые идут по собственным следам назад. Доверься путям, людям и принципам, которые никогда тебя не предавали.

text.ru:
Небодержатель

Звёзды склоняют нас, но не связывают.

Небодержатель, какие труды тяготят тебя? Я вижу тебя под сводом звёздного неба, чей край лежит на твоих плечах. Твоё лицо искривлено и посерело от усилия, дыхание напряжено… Мили лазурита, индиго и аметиста переходят в обсидиан, усыпанные серебряными точками — одни живые, другие призрачные, — красными шарами, опалесцентными туманами, всё это удерживается на твоей маленькой, согнутой фигуре.

Я вижу, что ты не обращаешь на меня внимания: даже мгновение рассеянности, думаешь ты, станет концом всего. Сферы перестанут звучать согласно; арабеска времени прекратит писать себя; мороз покорит морскую зыбь и плодовые сады. Твоя рука, с побелевшими костяшками, сжимает дрожащие струны судьбы: ты боишься, что те, кого ты любишь, выскользнут, дрейфуя в энтропию, стоит тебе отпустить их хоть на миг…

О Невесомый, услышь меня: небеса не рухнут, если ты их отпустишь. Оставь страх, расправь спину. Позволь тверди соскользнуть с твоих плеч и покатиться по небу, где ей и место. То, чему суждено вращаться, будет вращаться без твоей команды.

Вдохни и дай тетиве своего тела ослабнуть. Выдохни в ночь:

Que sera sera — что будет, то будет.

Que sera sera — что будет, то будет.

Небодержатель, легко раздражаться, если страх не исчезает сразу после отпускания. Нас учили гнаться, торопиться, прикреплять любимое железной скобой. Когда что-то ускользает, хватка становится крепче. Но принять может только открытая рука.

Мир движется без тебя — но благодаря тебе он сияет.
text.en:
Sky Bearer

The stars incline us, they do not bind us.

Star-Bearer, what toils beset you? I see you beneath the vault of starry sky, its edge resting on your shoulders. Your visage is gnarled and ashen from the effort, your breath strained… Miles of lapis lazuli, indigo, amethyst, fade into obsidian, peppered with silver pinpricks—some living, others spectral— red orbs, opalescent mists, all balanced on your small and crouching form.

I see you pay me no mind; even a moment of distraction would punctuate the end of all, you think: the spheres will fail to harmonize; the arabesque of time will cease to write itself; frost will conquer the sea’s sway and teeming orchards. Your hand, its knuckles blanched, is clasping at the trembling strings of fate—you fear those you love will slip away, drift into entropy, if only you let go for a moment…

O Weightless One, hear me now: The heavens won’t collapse if you release them. Abandon fear, unfurl your spine. Let the firmament slide from your shoulders and roll across the sky where it belongs. What is destined will turn without your command.

Breathe, and let the bowstring of your body slacken. Exhale into the night:

Que sera sera, whatever will be, will be.

It is tempting, Bearer, to be vexed if fear does not dissolve upon letting go. We learn to chase, to rush, to fasten to what we love with an iron shackle. When something slips away, our grip tightens. But only an open hand can receive.

The world does not rest upon our shoulders. Its weight was only ever the measure of your love. You mistook your devotion for gravity itself. It’s not the world pressing upon you, but your own heart in orbit. Now, step aside and watch the stars keep turning.

The world does move without you—but because of you, it shines.

quote.ru: Звёзды склоняют нас, но не связывают.
quote.en: The stars incline us, they do not bind us.

ru: Небодержатель, какие труды тяготят тебя? Я вижу тебя под сводом звёздного неба, чей край лежит на твоих плечах. Твоё лицо искривлено и посерело от усилия, дыхание напряжено… Мили лазурита, индиго и аметиста переходят в обсидиан, усыпанные серебряными точками — одни живые, другие призрачные, — красными шарами, опалесцентными туманами, всё это удерживается на твоей маленькой, согнутой фигуре.

Я вижу, что ты не обращаешь на меня внимания: даже мгновение рассеянности, думаешь ты, станет концом всего. Сферы перестанут звучать согласно; арабеска времени прекратит писать себя; мороз покорит морскую зыбь и плодовые сады. Твоя рука, с побелевшими костяшками, сжимает дрожащие струны судьбы: ты боишься, что те, кого ты любишь, выскользнут, дрейфуя в энтропию, стоит тебе отпустить их хоть на миг…

О Невесомый, услышь меня: небеса не рухнут, если ты их отпустишь. Оставь страх, расправь спину. Позволь тверди соскользнуть с твоих плеч и покатиться по небу, где ей и место. То, чему суждено вращаться, будет вращаться без твоей команды.
Вдохни и дай тетиве своего тела ослабнуть. Выдохни в ночь:
en: Star-Bearer, what toils beset you? I see you beneath the vault of starry sky, its edge resting on your shoulders. Your visage is gnarled and ashen from the effort, your breath strained… Miles of lapis lazuli, indigo, amethyst, fade into obsidian, peppered with silver pinpricks—some living, others spectral— red orbs, opalescent mists, all balanced on your small and crouching form.

I see you pay me no mind; even a moment of distraction would punctuate the end of all, you think: the spheres will fail to harmonize; the arabesque of time will cease to write itself; frost will conquer the sea’s sway and teeming orchards. Your hand, its knuckles blanched, is clasping at the trembling strings of fate—you fear those you love will slip away, drift into entropy, if only you let go for a moment…

O Weightless One, hear me now: The heavens won’t collapse if you release them. Abandon fear, unfurl your spine. Let the firmament slide from your shoulders and roll across the sky where it belongs. What is destined will turn without your command.
Breathe, and let the bowstring of your body slacken. Exhale into the night:

pullquote.ru: Que sera sera — что будет, то будет.
pullquote.en: Que sera sera, whatever will be, will be.

Мир движется без тебя — но благодаря тебе он сияет.

text.en: Card text in English.

Celestial Heir

The roots which bind also nourish.

Some rifts can only be bridged by ancestral ties, Celestial Heir. Your path leads to a threshold. You drift through dunes of lilac fog, your steps barely stirring the russet loam underfoot. Now and then, a familiar shape surfaces in the corner of your eye. You seem to recognize the gait, the posture, the half-turned face. Before you say a word, the silhouette disappears. You walk on, led by premonition. Sometimes, silken mists reveal the outline of an ancient structure. Empty porticos. Moss-veiled caryatids holding nothing. The spire of a sunken cathedral rising out of a faraway lake. You arrive at a cliff’s edge—the same somniferous fog churns below. Some distance beyond, a strange staircase begins. Woven from gilded bones, it winds upward and vanishes. You sense that you must ascend it. Yet, how to reach the first step? Celestial Heir, some questions cannot be answered by one mind alone.

To proceed, seek guidance from your elders.

Call forth your ancestors. Take inspiration from the tale of long-lost Odysseus, who turned to the dead for direction. A sacrificial offering to the ravenous specters unclouded their eyes, filmed with the ash of forgetting. Fresh blood stirs ancient memories to life again. It closed the gap between the present and the past, granting Odysseus an audience with the blind prophet Tiresias.

Celestial Heir, when the path is invisible, step into the tracks left behind by your forerunners: Revive the wisdom of your parents, grandparents, and beyond. Family is not limited to your immediate relatives: look to your community, both present and past, to tradition, to stories passed down through generations. Probe both the pithy wisdom of aphorisms and the obscure symbolism of myth. Ask your parents for recollections of situations resembling yours. Notice the thread of their characters running through you, both its light and dark patches. Now is the time to resolve conflicts with family and understand their value. Remember, mistakes can be as instructive as victories.

A newly-minted journeyman sees a steep cliff where mountain dwellers see a staircase. Oftentimes novelty disguises itself as a riddle, yet the solution to it is one step away. Let others’ experience—accrued over decades and centuries—point you in the right direction.

text.en: Card text in English.

Perennial Calligrapher

In the eye of the storm, a master’s hand stays steady.

Time halts for you, Perennial Calligrapher! Step outside its breathless kaleidoscope, into the clarity of your craft. The instinct of hurry lifts up swarms of restless thoughts. Like bits of sand raised from the river bottom by rushing feet, they muddy the waters of your mind. In the eddies of disquiet, you cannot even see the terrain of your fingertips. Let worries fall to the ground like last summer’s cicada husks. Feel the hands of countless clocks ossify and halt. In the silence outside of time, summon the alchemical abilities of your soul. Close your eyes and whisper:

I distill.

Gather your tools about you, Calligrapher. Let miles of parchment pass under your brush. Think not of the pristine pages that lie ahead, nor of the ink-lined pages you leave behind. The past and future are abstractions—penumbrae cast across the present. They are indeed lush, gilded with what could have been and what could still be. Yet they accomplish naught but the withering of what is.

Blind to life itself, the eye grows dull studying phantoms. The mind, well-traveled in the realms of fancy, returns to an unskilled hand, an empty page, and a dried-up inkwell. The moment will come when you emerge from your study, trading the company of ancient scrolls for the Babelian babble of crowds. Now is the time to hone your craft.

Remember that the world began from craft: word made flesh, loam made life. Out of the nebulous body of Chaos, craftsman gods raised sublime peaks and mercurial waters, lush woods and creatures of the earth. From the dark glyph of soil, they carved people—inspired with a fragment of divine breath and possessed of hands that will continue their legacy.

Calligrapher, master your craft, without hurry or vanity. Humbly accept that creativity unfolds in due time. Evolution cannot be rushed: every turn it takes will add to the richness of its design. With an attentive mind, your creations will be flawless. Concentrate on your work—keep a clear, steady focus.

All that matters now gathers in the space between you, your page, and your purpose, Calligrapher. The purity of the present moment holds the key to creation.

text.en:
Mind Soother

Sometimes, the eye is an inward-facing mirror.

All around you, things betray their usual form, Mind Soother. The angles of buildings sharpen and crowd together. Streets shift and close off at random. Teeming thoroughfares appear in the place of quiet alleyways. The birdsong you awoke to every day has disappeared—the air swells with a deep machinic rumble. Gloaming has shed its hazy pastels. Your apartment walls issue melancholy creaks, as though your home were a ship sailing through the heavy ink of night. In the faltering glow of a swaying lightbulb, you murmur to yourself: why is everything falling apart?

The chaos you see in the world is a reflection of your mind.

Your thoughts careen through waves of panic, jumbling familiar fragments into an ominous mosaic. The body, possessed by inner turbulence, sees naught but its own enervated image reflected in the cityscape. To stop the world’s sinister transformation, you must restore serenity within your mind. Ancient wisdom can teach you how.

One day, while the Buddha and his disciples were traveling from village to village, he felt thirsty, and asked a disciple to fetch some water from a nearby stream. The disciple found the usually clear stream muddied, for it so happened that two bullock carts had passed through it recently. The disciple returned empty-handed, explaining that the water was unfit to drink. The Buddha sent him back to the stream, insisting that he fetch some water in any case. To the disciple’s surprise, the stream had cleared entirely, and the water he returned with this time was pure. “It is the same with the mind, also,” the Buddha said. “To stop the circulation of restless thoughts, one must wait for them to settle on their own. Effort will only churn the mind’s waters.”

Mind Soother, to calm your thoughts, turn your attention elsewhere. Read a book, meet a friend. Walk, dance, cry, sleep. Inhabit your body to your utmost ability, using breath to measure time. Feel the air around you with your skin. Seek warmth and comfort. Watch the flight of birds trace poems to the sky. Your thoughts will quiet on their own, and you will find the world reflecting your regenerated calm back to you.

text.en: Card text in English.
Card text in English.

Cautious Weaver

The lightest thread can bridge an abyss.

Pray watch your step, Cautious Weaver. You stand before a chasm. The bridge across is lithe, kin to a sinuous cherry bough. It bends and sways; it will not snap, but it might cast you off. Do not tarry, yet tread carefully. Let caution chisel your force into a tool.

Make use of all your senses. Listen not only with your ears, but with your skin. Read the grain beneath your feet; be faithful to the slightest change in texture. When the path ahead is unsteady, remember the spider’s art:

Lay threads carefully along the way. Test each line. Let their tremor guide you.

So did the Shamans of the Limit, who, aeons ago, kept their vigil at the Rim of Birds, where the mountains are the teeth of sky gnawing the earth. The sky and ground close upon each other like grinding jaws, shrieking with wind, then open by a hair’s width again, as they have since time immemorial. Through the brief crack between them fly two flocks: white cranes, bearing unborn souls into breath, and the black swans carrying the dead to the upper realms. Those who mistime the passage are crushed; their bones whiten the slopes like snow.

The shamans waited there to rescue what slipped between—a breath half-born, a memory unmoored. They wove gossamer nets of comet hair, for only such filaments could hold a soul. When the crack widened, they cast their shining webs across the wind and caught the lost in their meshes, to weave them back into the world’s design. Some called them Arachna’s priests, for they tended the same art the first Spider taught when she stitched the edges of sky and earth together in threads of light. Over their gate, the Shamans of the Limit carved a single law:

Finesse conquers force.

So they knelt at the edge, palms on the trembling rock, listening for the moment when the jaws of heaven sigh apart—and then stepped forward, spider-light, to pluck a soul from the storm.

Learn from them, Weaver; their art is yours to echo. Whether weaving word, alliance, or design, let delicacy steer your strength. Bind lightly—alliances, hints, and sidelong smiles. Keep your intent bold, your touch precise. Spend no coin on bravado; choose your words as if each were silk.

Move like a miniaturist. This moment is your parchment, the medium forbids crude strokes. With prudence for gloves and patience for brush, draw the bridge just as you cross it; every stroke a step, every breath a foothold.

Precision is the gentlest form of power.

text.en: Card text in English.
Card text in English.
Card text in English.

Pearl Diver

He who dives into the sea of affliction brings up rare pearls.

I see you sinking, O bewitched pearl diver! I see you looking through a glass, darkly. Your days are void. A sense of insidious affliction comes over you, as if your veins are infused with torpor. It is the future entering into you, transforming itself within your shrouded heart long before it happens.

Resistance is futile. Embrace your solitude, and love it. In the somniferous sea, become the mystery at the crossroads of your senses—and the meaning therein. If the wayward stars have ceased to guide you, say to the waters:

I flow.

Sink willingly, or be dragged by the undines, whose arms are seafoam and hair like dregs of darkness. Those who surrender will be granted passage to the sea’s vast emerald soul. It is woven from gossamer mirrors that hold the perished beauty and forgotten faces of the past. It’s said that all things lost under the sun can be recovered here: forgone loves, forfeit dreams, forsaken paths. Yet the haunted mirrors are so fragile they evaporate at a touch—unless the lost thing still belongs to you.

Now gently hear and kindly judge my scrying: You will embrace the fall. Sink deep into the Sea of Shifting Mirrors and reclaim the one that holds your disavowed treasure. You’ll know it by the strange reflections of two faces—your killer and your healer. When you accept that both are yours, it will begin to shine. Then, take it. Own it.

Hark now, what harmony is this? From castles built of bone, a marvelous music issues. Can you not feel the whale song reverberating in the nacreous waters, echoed by chanting stars? They sing for you of strange delights that make life pungent and intoxicating. You are being summoned to the surface.

Crowned with curiously carven jewels, re-emerge victorious. But lo! The gifts of the abyss melt in your hands like phantom crystals smuggled from a dream. Be not beguiled—you drank them like a dissolved pearl. The sea now ebbs and flows in your blood, a rhythmic reassuring sound. It is the sound of mystery that beats at the heart of reality, and you are right within it. When you go under, you become the interval between two trembling notes—and the music of the spheres goes on, beautiful.

quote.ru: Тот, кто ныряет в море скорби, поднимает со дна редкие жемчужины.
quote.en: He who dives into the sea of affliction brings up rare pearls.

en: I see you sinking, O bewitched pearl diver! I see you looking through a glass, darkly. Your days are void. A sense of insidious affliction comes over you, as if your veins are infused with torpor. It is the future entering into you, transforming itself within your shrouded heart long before it happens.

Resistance is futile. Embrace your solitude, and love it. In the somniferous sea, become the mystery at the crossroads of your senses—and the meaning therein. If the wayward stars have ceased to guide you, say to the waters:

pullquote.en: I flow.

en: Sink willingly, or be dragged by the undines, whose arms are seafoam and hair like dregs of darkness. Those who surrender will be granted passage to the sea’s vast emerald soul. It is woven from gossamer mirrors that hold the perished beauty and forgotten faces of the past. It’s said that all things lost under the sun can be recovered here: forgone loves, forfeit dreams, forsaken paths. Yet the haunted mirrors are so fragile they evaporate at a touch—unless the lost thing still belongs to you.

Now gently hear and kindly judge my scrying: You will embrace the fall. Sink deep into the Sea of Shifting Mirrors and reclaim the one that holds your disavowed treasure. You’ll know it by the strange reflections of two faces—your killer and your healer. When you accept that both are yours, it will begin to shine. Then, take it. Own it.

text.en:
Sleepwalker

The stars see what the sun cannot.

Stranger, whither are you bound? With a sun-glazed gaze and in a pilgrim's raiment retaining the dust and mire of so many devious ways? Have you not chased mirages long enough? Those illusions may glitter, yet they are hollow whimsy, born of blazing light. But wait for dusk. The stars are winking you a message in interstellar semaphore:

Not all truths are sharp, and not all paths are straight.

Hear me: seek not the sun’s clarity in dunes unrolling endlessly, like scrolls of scorched papyrus. A daylight wanderer sees the sand-wastes as they are—vast, dry, and forlorn. But a night-time drifter sees the desert as it might be—a theater of miracles under the vault of an ebony sky. The desert breathes. All things are cloaked for a while in mystery—and then transformed. The slopes roll as the sea, the thistles turn to stars, the skies into a crystal chalice where vague moons are lost amid great nenuphars.

The desert does not yell its truths. It murmurs them in the spaces between your steps and in the silent interludes between your thoughts. Its arcane language cannot be deciphered, put on the rack of scrutiny and tortured for its secrets. Sleepwalker, know that sometimes it is not the sleep of reason that breeds monsters, but reason’s unrelenting, sleepless stare.

Let your eyes soften; make no haste. Just follow the clues in strange, enchanting writings all around you—in wind-drawn arabesques and serpent-trails between the dunes, in stones shaped like shards of dreams, and in luminous specters slowly dancing through the maze of stars.

Hear me, for I will speak: Demand no clear aim; relinquish urgency. Don’t be afraid to be confused. Try to remain confused. Just keep sleepwalking—watch your dreams. When you return to waking life, forgive yourself for not taking action. Remember you were here.

And while you were lulled by the gentle hiss of silence, night and time pouring through the hourglass of the immemorial desert, your soul wasn’t idle. It was spinning something of itself on an unseen wheel, something fine, elusive. Patience awhile. Trust in the subtle miracle that’s woven inside you by stealth, while you are sleeping.

Card text in English.

Fear Conqueror

The darkest night is behind the closed eye.

Something stirs in these strange regions, Fear Conqueror. The night is starless—a lone sickle-moon, its edges tinged with crimson, interrupts the onyx sky. Veiled by a sallow aura, it’s an omen of storms. Hastily, you make your way through a tangle of alleys. You meet no one. A few stray windows glow with a distant, myopic light, barely allaying your solitude. Your footsteps leave a trail of faint echoes. You throw a quick glance over your shoulder. In the darkness between the small islands of light cast by the street-lamps, vagueness begins to take shape. You quicken your pace, yet it seems closer every time you look behind. You can hear it now, but the sounds it issues are ever-changing: a heavy, dragging shuffle; claws scraping the cobblestone; the rustle of wings flapping. Fear Conqueror, rise against your desire to flee. Stand firm and turn around.

Facing your fear will shatter its spell.

Escape offers fecund soil for nightmares to thrive. Their power grows in the twilight zone, assuming terrifying forms. Something unseen can never be countered. Consider the vampire lurking in liminal spaces, between sleep and waking, shadow and candlelight. Slipping through the cracks of consciousness, he cannot be named, nor recalled. He remains a flicker, the effaced portion of a palimpsest beneath one’s waking thoughts. He is visible only in the withering of the victim; felt in the heaviness of one’s heart. Escaping his control is impossible—he is eternal and tenacious, and the night—his hunting ground—is ubiquitous. Yet being brought into the light will end him, literally and metaphorically.

Fear Conqueror, ask yourself what exactly you are afraid of. What is the worst possible outcome? What will happen when you live through it? How will your life look once you’ve crossed that bridge? Is the threat imminent? And, most importantly, what conjures up this fear when there isn’t any immediate threat on the horizon? Is it desire? Unfulfilled potential? Or inertia?

Sometimes, fear is a mere vestige, a dark corner from the past one never dared illuminate. Threats may be a shadow play, endlessly reenacting scenes from one’s early days. Such fears can only be banished by a spotlight. Do not be afraid to see them for what they are: as you inch closer to your fears, the power invested in them will return to you.

text.en:
Flawed Perfection

Mistakes are beats in the pulse of becoming.

Feel the vibrant pulse of your becoming, Flawed Perfection! You graze the hand of fate while you sculpt your life together. Moving in unison with destiny, you craft moments of plenitude, awash with the delight of a full moon observing herself in a still pond. Sometimes, you fall out of step with celestial motion, creating messy constellations, gritty and askew like freshwater pearls. As you live, you watch your words fall on deaf ears, the careful geometry of your plans shatter, your dreams crack in the kiln of reality. You may be tempted to hide your mishaps, camouflage old stains with fresh paint. Before you polish away your scuffs and cracks, fill them with gold.

Blemishes narrate the richness of your experience; they do not define you.

One of humanity’s many false gods is homogenous perfection. Faces without creases, objects without defect, and love without turmoil promise illusory safety. Like the image of paradise as an absence of suffering, uniform perfection annihilates chance. Yet with the suffering it precludes, it smothers pleasure. A face marked by joy may be spurned for its creased eyelids. It may be loved for them also—for the ability of skin to embody laughter, to transform a fleeting smile into an endearing monument to joy.

Perfection, love your flaws, for they liken you to life itself. Perfectionism rejects becoming and favors sterile being. Mistakes create experience, taste, and authenticity. Challenges offer improvement without end. Appreciate the change that imperfection calls for, and the richness of character that you accrue from it.

Centuries ago, one wise man knew that the universe deals in diversity, not perfection. When the tea master Sen no Rikyū was a young man and studied under the tutelage of the master Takeno Jōō, he was instructed to tidy up a garden as an entrance exam. After the garden had been polished to perfection, Sen no Rikyū shook a tree, and a few leaves drifted down to the ground. This small flaw imbued the garden with a lifelike charm. He would go on to infuse the tea ceremony with the principle behind this simple act—the beauty of transience and imperfection.

Dulcet Glimmer

Gratitude is the echo of the divine in the human soul.

When you speak, the universe softly listens, Dulcet Glimmer. It scatters secret notes across your path. A helping hand, a warm corner, a beautiful flower… Sometimes, the prose of daily life turns to poetry, and you begin to see the patterns of a language humans can sense but never master. With serendipity for a voice, it illuminates the play of light and shadow casting its unique relief across your life. Humans have countless ways of answering this voice, or calling out to it in an hour of need. Offerings strewn at the feet of sculptures and gold-plated images, incense burned in ritual braziers, hours of silent prayer. As a wise teacher once said, a wordless “thank you” is nevertheless enough.

Gratitude opens possibilities that movement and effort cannot.

There is an old story of a wise teacher and his two disciples who sought enlightenment under a sprawling banyan tree. Both disciples wished to know when they would finally transcend the cycle of death and rebirth. To the first student, the teacher said that he would need to live another twenty lives until his spirit could achieve liberation. Frustrated, the young man cried out: “How can I wait that long?” To the second disciple, the teacher said that he would have to live as many lives as the banyan tree has leaves before his spirit could cast off the burden of the transient. Delighted with the assurance that he would one day be free, the second disciple leapt into rapturous dance, and achieved enlightenment at that very moment.

Dulcet Glimmer, there is wisdom and richness in gratitude: Allow memories of kindness, relief, and luck to pour into your mind. Think of those who have been good to you, of words that have warmed your soul. Think of beautiful things that gladdened your eye. Remember the momentous and the miniscule, the purposeful and accidental. Give thanks to the transcendent and the mortal, to the universe and to the people around you.

Reside in gratitude—hold a gentle, nourishing light in the delicate gauze of your soul. No detail is too small to be grateful for. Even the joy of seeing a dot snail climb a tuft of moss can be a gift when welcomed with an open heart. A soul that smiles at the little wonders of the world is a treasure in its own right.

Horizon Seeker

The compass points to a place big enough for a human heart.

Yearning moves you, Horizon Seeker! Your gaze is fixed on distant sierras, crowned with dawn’s iridescence. Your heart has grown too vast for these narrow city streets. Whispers melt on your eardrums with hopeless insignificance. Your words burst through the needlework of propriety like steel gauntlets. You have no patience for people’s little ways. Quotidian life is a prison of unwritten rules. Torn between burgeoning purpose and a lack of direction, you wander familiar routes like a caged tiger. Horizon Seeker, it is time to take a bird’s-eye view.

Find high ground to glimpse your destination.

You scale the city walls. As you reach the top, the vista takes your breath away. Tessellated fields chafe against serpentine rivers. Downslope winds comb the green hair of forests and toy with the windvanes of faraway settlements.

Horizon Seeker, look into the distance and take aim. Your journey begins now. On your way, you will find islands of peace and pleasure—make sure not to forget the reason for your journey. As long as each step you take is filled with purpose, all roads will lead to your lodestar.

Perhaps, an old tale will help along the way. Centuries ago, a lone bamboo cutter stumbled across a strangely luminescent bamboo stalk. Slicing it open, he was stunned to find a miniscule infant at its core. The bamboo cutter and his wife adopted the mysterious infant, who grew into a dazzling woman within mere months. Suitors began to crowd around their abode, enchanted by her ethereal beauty, but all were rejected. Five nobles vied for her heart; she gave each an impossible task to deter further advances. From one, she requested the stone begging bowl of the Buddha, from another, a robe of fire rat skins, from the next one a jewel from a dragon’s neck, and so on. The Emperor himself asked for her hand, yet was turned down, for she always knew where she belonged. Her gaze was fixed on the full moon. It summoned her. To her worried parents, she revealed that she was not of this world. She was a Moon Princess, damned to experience material attachment for a past crime. After the Moon Maiden served her sentence, an embassy of heavenly beings descended to welcome her back. Leaving mementoes for her family and the emperor, she donned a feather robe, and her earthly bonds dissolved. Ascending to the sky, she took her place among the stars.

text.en:
Perennial Herb

The key to the city is hidden in the ruins.

Sometimes the castles you’ve built in the sky come crumbling down, Perennial Herb. It’s a peculiar pain, seeing the makings of history become the husks of lost dreams. Heartsick, you wander through the ruins. Questions pierce your heart like poison arrows. “Was I not good enough? Was my hope but delusion? Can I still rescue something from the wreckage?” Perennial Herb, hold the broken pieces gently. Meet them without flinching.

Harvest the kernels of wisdom within your defeat.

Failure may feel like a kind of death, yet it is the very source of lifeblood. A joyless patch of life is like a drought. In rainless seasons, the roots of plants burrow ever deeper. To an ignorant eye, they seem withered, waiting for rain. Yet far down in subterranean depths, they are quietly designing a foundation for ages. Reaching ever deeper, they find enduring nourishment in underground streams. No longer will the weather’s whims determine their survival. Thus, in a wilderness where a well-cared-for plant would perish, wild herbs persevere.

Perennial Herb, to grow from this loss, you must cease trying to fix it. In order to become more resilient, you must resist the urge to exit the discomfort of defeat. Dissect the events. Instead of self-flagellating, try clarity: How much of it was your responsibility? Which factors were external to your control? What could you do differently next time? Remember: not all failure is the result of a mistake. Sometimes, one does the right thing at the wrong time.

Once, a man named Pien Ho discovered uncut jade in the Ch'u Hills. Sensing beauty beyond its prosaic exterior, he offered it as a gift to the Emperor. To the Emperor it seemed but an ordinary stone, so he condemned Ho as a liar, and had his right foot amputated. Some time passed, and another Emperor ascended to the throne. Again, Pien Ho submitted the jade as a gift. Again, he was condemned as a liar, and his left foot was cut off. Ho cried for three days and three nights. The depths of his sorrow prompted the Emperor to ask why he was so dejected—Ho’s punishment was no rarity at the time. “I am lamenting that one mistakes a precious gem for an ordinary stone, and an honest man for a liar,” Ho replied. Perplexed, the Emperor ordered a jeweler to polish the jade. Finally, it became clear to all that Pien Ho had discovered a treasure. Humility is the patience of jade waiting for its time to shine.

quote.ru: Ключ от города спрятан в руинах.
quote.en: The key to the city is hidden in the ruins.

en: Sometimes the castles you’ve built in the sky come crumbling down, Perennial Herb. It’s a peculiar pain, seeing the makings of history become the husks of lost dreams. Heartsick, you wander through the ruins. Questions pierce your heart like poison arrows. “Was I not good enough? Was my hope but delusion? Can I still rescue something from the wreckage?” Perennial Herb, hold the broken pieces gently. Meet them without flinching.

pullquote.en: Harvest the kernels of wisdom within your defeat.

text.en:
Illusion Reader

The crooked branch casts a straight shadow.

What mysteries puzzle you, Illusion Reader? Some corners on the map of life are vertiginous enough to unravel the mind. There are people whose eyes are funhouse mirrors, twisting your own reflection into peculiar shapes. There are places untouched by the laws of reality, where waterfalls flow upside down and the trees are studded with rhinestones. There are odd days when every arriving message is written in code and lost objects turn up in unexpected places. When the fabric of reality uncoils, one gets a closer look at the thread it is woven from. Life shows you its ambiguous facets not to drown you in confusion, but to open your eyes.

Ambivalence keeps the mind supple.

Seeing the world with absolute certainty means one is tethered in place. Cause and effect clips a single strand from a plethora of possibilities and names it the root cause. Held in the rigid grasp of its logic, humans look upon the world as though with blinders, seeing a slender path in place of endless vistas.

Illusion Reader, have you heard the story of the empty boat? A long time ago, a monk meditated in a boat gliding gently upon a lake. Hours passed, and his mind fell silent. Suddenly, he was shaken from his trance by the impact of another boat against his. The monk’s heart flooded with frustration, and he turned around furiously to face the culprit. “Why would you disturb my meditation?” he exclaimed. A strange sight greeted him: an empty boat, which must have become unfastened from the dock and drifted out into the lake. His anger changed to confusion and finally to enlightenment—one’s life path is rife with collisions, yet it is the assumption of ill intent which makes them so distressing. Collisions are impersonal, the monk realized, it’s the interpretations we endow them with that feed our anger.

Illusion Reader, open your mind to the revelations offered by strangeness. When a situation seems confusing, ask yourself whether it is inviting you to shift your perspective. The same shadow can look like a bunny or a bird, depending on one’s way of seeing. A conflict can be a confirmation of one’s fears, or a clash with an empty boat. Don’t be so sure you’re seeing the whole picture — or even the right one. Perspective is a game — play it.

text.en: Card text in English.

Wandering Hermit

The hermit’s heart is small, but the world enters through its window.

Our paths have crossed again, Wandering Hermit. Deep in the wilderness, you walk alone, guided by the slow pulse of stars and your lantern’s wavering glow. A kaleidoscope of landscapes is etched into your eyes. Ancient forests festooned with arabesques of ivy and lichens. Spires of sunken cities glimpsed between obsidian dunes. Three-pronged waterfalls buttressed by ancient mossy stones. You long for a familiar harbor: a lighted window, the warm rustle of a conversation. Yet before you mistake your solitude for a bad omen, remember:

On a lonely path, you will find yourself.

Have you heard of the recluse in the Bamboo Grove? He was a court scholar who had renounced public life to live among bamboo. When asked why trade ambition for solitude, he replied: “The bamboo bends but does not break. I wish the same for my mind.” Sometimes, the path to wisdom is as short as a curious glance at the surrounding world. The soft yet deliberate play of cats who land on their feet. The hardy humbleness of moss at home on the steepest of cliffs. The flexible determination of a sprout emerging from rocky soil.

Other times, your mentor is your self, for a pebble in solitude becomes a mountain in thought. The wilderness is not exile, but a rite of passage. In solitude, the self is born again. Sudden adversity faced alone reveals what you are made of. With no one to lean on, ingenuity awakens and will is fortified. To reach the windswept heights of freedom, one must cross steep cliffs and sublime storms.

Take heart, Hermit: This long and winding road leads to your core. Remember, the soul travels at the speed of a camel. You will encounter things that only you can understand. Learn to untangle the unknown. Find the roots of what moves you. Company now would cloud your sight.

In the absence of conversation, you will hear creation. A human word is a fragment, a blind man’s cane feeling along the outlines of the great silence in which the divine speaks. As white—a seeming absence—is all colors at once, so silence is the plenitude of voice. Solitude will tune your heartstrings; they will reverberate with the unspoken once again.

text.en:
Blithe Juggler

The worlds turn not by force, but by the grace of a light touch.

What a beautiful day, Blithe Juggler, to roam the world with an open heart and adventure on your mind! Flocks of white clouds gambol across a cerulean sky; mountains seem within reach. Roadside flowers sway in the breeze of your gait. From the bend ahead come voices, the clatter of wheels, and laughter—another marvel of the day. Before you step into the thick of it, let your heart smile and say:

I play.

In the bustle, you’ll want to hold to your companions—your triumphs, loves, and callings. Remember: life is made of curious matter. Hold it like a feather—with open palms, unafraid to toss aloft—and it gleams, weightless. Grip it tight, and it turns to iron.

Even the gods knew that. Before time began, there was only stillness—no weight, no gravity, just tension before motion. Then the Juggler of Worlds lifted a flute to unseen lips and breathed a sonorous note. It rippled through the void. Sound became wind, wind became dust, and dust began to dance. The Juggler sculpted it into spheres and spun them into play. He laughed and tossed them higher—each toss a planet, a moon, a life. The galaxies turned not from toil but from delight in motion.

The iatromantes say the universe still vibrates with that pulse. The hum alerts the bee, trembles the wild rose, halts the woodpecker mid-strike. Every heart beats to the Juggler’s rhythm—lifted, let go, returned in grace.

Hear me, for I am so old I once knew the secret of the universe—but have since forgotten, just as you forget that you are made of stardust. You are part of that continuing toss of light and laughter. For a brief instant you stand amid the spinning worlds, astonished at this tumble into being—as if some celestial alembic burst, scattering a pyramid, an orchid, a glass harmonica, an aepyornis, a jade Buddha, an orchestra, a labradoodle, and these very letters you are reading now.

O wonder!

You are a spark in the cosmic juggling, your life a bright bubble in mid-air. Live well. Live gratefully. Be convinced of the essential goodness of the universe. Go fill your eyes with wonder. Stay open, then open more. Learn things. And remember: knowledge is not an answer to be grasped but a mystery to be lived. Let life happen to you—beauty and terror alike. Keep juggling lightly—let the spheres of your joys and heartbreaks spin through your hands. When one thing falls, another rises to replace it. Nothing is final.

text.en:
Trinket Master

Memory sweeps through the mind’s palace like a busy broom.

Your sanctuary is a microcosm of the universe, Trinket Master. Curiosities collected on distant travels line the shelves. Sifting through an ancient window grille, dull sunlight illuminates mysterious skeletons, varicolored flasks, crumbling scrolls, and bottle gardens. Each weathered parchment, each incantation-sealed chest, each mottled crystal whispers of a bygone adventure. An extended body, your brimming haven keeps the score of all your memories and iterations.

Yet after countless days of quests and nights spent probing the arcane, chaos reigns in your abode. Vials teeter haphazardly on the edges of overflowing shelves. Creeping vines braid together in crevices between dusty tomes and slouching candles. Rats and lizards ransack stacks of scribbled discoveries, and long-tongued akaname gather to feast on the grime-coated bathroom tiles. Without a proper place, your trinkets cannot testify to the richness of your life.

A vigorous tidying is due to restore your dwelling to its glory.

The act of cleaning transcends mere hygiene and becomes a threshold between worlds. Many traditions reflect this. Chinese families swept away last year’s misfortunes before the Lunar New Year. Hindus polished their homes to welcome the goddess Lakshmi during Diwali. In Zen monasteries, sweeping and washing are part of daily practice — clearing space before meditation, silence, and insight. Dust and grime harbor stagnant energies, misfortunes, and spent time. The broom is a ritual tool, the act of sweeping—a form of prayer. Fire clears away last year’s chaff to make way for new beginnings.

Trinket Master, your chores are themselves an incantation: In moments of vigorous scrubbing and dusting, you purify liminal spaces where the human and divine meet, inviting blessings, prosperity, and benevolent spirits to cross your threshold. Let each object find its place, fill your rooms with fresh air, and scrape the soot of years off your precious belongings. Laxity invites unwelcome visitors—whether skeletons rattling in grimy closets or misfortunes. Cleaning restores order both to your space and your self.

As each forgotten bibelot passes through your hands, you will recall a fragment of the past. Some of these revelations will be trivial, and others deeply meaningful. After weeks of forays into the unknown, a return to the self is essential. Tidy up your home to remember who you are.

text.en: Card text in English.

Brave Heart

Every path started with one first step.

This view is enough to chill the breath, Brave Heart! You stand at the edge of a vast monolith, dripping in scented herbs. A long journey has led you to this region, and you must cross the vast ravine to continue. You’ve seen countless records of an enchanted bridge that appears when one steps off this cliff, yet you hesitate. Brave Heart, sometimes you must trust your path before you can see it.

Leap boldly into the unknown.

Brave Heart, have you heard the story of Hanuman’s leap of faith? Half-human and half-monkey, Hanuman was the son of the god of wind and air, and a loyal devotee of legendary prince Rama. Hanuman was blessed by several gods: Brahma gave him the ability to alter his body size at will, Agni granted him protection from fire, Varuna—from water, and his father Vayu made him as fast as the wind. When Hanuman was young, he used his powers to cause mischief, and was cursed by the gods to forget them. Thus, he lived ignorant of his strength until one fateful quest.

When Rama’s wife Sita was kidnapped by the ten-headed demon-king, Rama summoned Hanuman for help. After a long search, Hanuman discovered that Sita was trapped on the demon king’s island, on the other end of the ocean. Hanuman would have to leap over the ocean to save her, yet its vastness made him stall. He deliberated, dejected about his odds. His warriors faltered, also. After a long pause, the immortal bear-king stepped up. “Have you forgotten who you are, Hanuman?” Realizing his long-forgotten divine powers, Hanuman took the leap. Trees were uprooted and the earth trembled as he took off. Cutting through the air like a spear, he landed on the island, and rescued Sita.

Brave Heart, triumph lies within your inner strength, not in endless deliberation. When done in excess, planning becomes delay and, finally, surrender. Waiting until one is ready is a paradox—the skills to master a situation arrive in the process of living through it. Often, people lose themselves in the analysis of possible pitfalls, forgetting that humans were designed to withstand and overcome the unexpected. Trust in yourself and in life. Take the leap.

text.en: Card text in English.
Card text in English.

String Slicer

Through a slit in the chrysalis, a butterfly glimpses the sky.

Whose hand directs the music of your life, String Slicer? You cannot help but dance to its subliminal melody. On this stage, you are both performer and audience, watching your own familiar routine. You know each chord, each movement—they are always the same. A haunting waterfall of notes cascades over the darkened stage. Your body liquefies, melting into a trembling backbend. A staccato erupts from the orchestra’s shadowy pit and you advance downstage. Suddenly, a trapdoor opens and you fall in, dangling from the edge. You are not afraid—this has happened before. Yet the perpetual repetition itself is gnawing dread. Is this all there is to your existence?

Question the prime mover to set yourself free.

As you look around, searching for the source of your perennial dance, you see something glisten. Translucent strings tied to every joint of your body disappear in the murk above. It is their impalpable pull that orchestrates the monotony. Before you resign yourself to mechanized movement, know that this discovery was the first step to freedom. In miniature, it twins the tale of how humans emerged from underground.

At the dawn of humanity, the first people lived in chthonic depths. Unbeknownst to them, the world above could be reached by way of a steep incline through a narrow, humid corridor lined with speleothems. The sun’s movement outside cast shifting shapes across the cave walls. The cave dwellers believed that the shadows’ play comprised the universe. One day, those most curious set out to explore the path leading out of the cave. When they first saw true sunlight, they shrunk from it. The vivid green pastures and the azure of the sky seemed garish and monstrous compared to the soft gray of shadows. Yet they persevered, and when they saw the night sky, their sacrifice paled in comparison to the breathtaking beauty of the sky’s jewels.

Slicer, break the strings that bind you: Pause and examine your influences. Are your desires truly your own? Are the standards you strive for aligned with your dreams? Are your actions true to your current self, or vestiges of the past? Once you see the strings, all that remains is to sever them. Do not fear uncertainty—your freedom begins now.

text.en:
Tender Sprout

Delicately, new life stirs within you.

The world welcomes you back, Tender Sprout! You were born from the vestiges of shattered promise. When mourning ended and the petals of lost hopes fell away, you surrendered to forgetful slumber. Now the warm glow of possibility laps against the darkness behind your lidded eyes. It beckons:

Reach softly for new beginnings.

Past grief has chipped away at your shell, but the radiance within you is indestructible. Still, it needs tenderness to thrive. Tread gently while you fortify yourself anew. Consider the wisdom of flowers: before unfolding their silken blooms, they must first push through soil. Fragile shoots evade stones and find nourishment. Their budding strength grows quietly, guided by the promise of distant sunlight. Obstacles do not deter them; they nimbly grow around them.

Consider the wisdom of Zhuangzi, who wrote of beginnings not as moments of arrival, but as quiet shifts in condition: Seeds have tiny sprouts. When they enter water, they form thin skins. At the edge of water and land, they take on the covering of frogs. On hills and mounds, they become plantain. Plantain, when nourished, becomes crow’s-foot. The leaves of crow’s-foot turn into butterflies. Butterflies change into insects that resemble snakes. After many days, these insects become birds. The birds’ saliva turns into flies, which couple with bamboo long without shoots. The bamboo gives rise to the leopard, the leopard to the horse, the horse to the human. The human returns again to the tiny seeds. From the tiny seeds, things begin again.

O Tender Sprout, move like a nascent lotus. In still ponds, lotus seeds sink into the silt. Silt presses down. The seed swells, splits, lets out a shoot upward. Heavy catfish pass above obscuring the dim sunlight. The stem hollows and stiffens as it rises, beckoned by the evanescent glow above. When it reaches the light, it opens. The flower does not resent the mud that fed it, nor remember the darkness it crossed. It just blooms.

There is no need to rush. Rise softly–your time will come to bloom. Little things are enough: quiet curiosity, a light desire. Let go of the withered past. Give your energy to that which is alive. The sun remembers every seed that waits in darkness.

text.en: Card text in English.
Card text in English.

Open Palm

Good and bad luck are the alternating footsteps of destiny.

Float lightly upon the tides of destiny, Open Palm! People are leaves on a river, sailing smoothly in a wide, languorous basin, only to be pulled to the bottom by a fickle rivulet. There is a secret rhythm governing the chaos, a shape to the movements of destiny. Yet the leaf knows neither where the river originates, nor where it connects to the sublime body of the sea. From within its foaming tumult, the river’s heterogeneous topography seems dreadful and meaningless. Instead of grasping at straws and seeking respite from its interminable flow, surrender to the movement.

Change is inevitable, discontent is a choice.

Imagine life as an endless rope sliding between your fingers. When time spent with people one loves, or circumstances that one relishes, come to an end, the first impulse commands: Hold them tight! Yet the only remaining thing is the wound born from holding on. Open Palm, do you know the tale of the Frontiersman who lost his horse?

Centuries ago, a man lived in a village verging on wilderness. One day, his horse ran away, disappearing deep into barbarian territory. His neighbors felt sorry for him, but his father said: “Who knows whether this is good or bad luck?” Some months later the horse returned, leading a band of noble barbarian horses. The neighbors congratulated the peasant, but his father again said, “Who knows whether this is good or bad luck?” Later, the peasant’s son fell while riding one of the wild horses and broke his leg. Again the neighbors pitied him; again, his father said, “Who knows whether this is good or bad luck?” One year later, barbarians invaded the country. Every adult man from the village went into battle—all except the peasant’s son, whose injury made him unfit for war. Only he survived in the end; everyone else perished…

Open Palm, learn to love fortune’s swinging pendulum: like stars amidst darkness and shadows during daytime, bad luck contains the seeds of good luck, and vice-versa. Life is defined by movement—accepting this will bring you peace. Don’t cling to the past—the tighter you hold it, the more painfully it will slip through your fingers. Trust that you are able to move in rhythm with the universe. Unclench your fingers, and the universe will hand you that which you truly need.

Remember: one pierced by life’s arrow suffers once, but one who dwells on the pain lances the same wound with a second arrow. To dwell on disquiet is to double it. To accept it is to soothe the pain while one awaits a change in fortune.

text.en: Card text in English.

Peace Maker

A buried sword sprouts roots and bears fruit.

Have you ever heard a mountain sigh, Peace Maker? Have you felt the sweet wind sift through a sun-drenched grove upon its unfurrowed slopes? How much beauty there is in softly dappled grass, in the rustle of a blackbird among the brambles. The serenity of titans is a self-contained cosmos, giving life to floral arabesques and the lives of fauna. This fertile calm is hard-won. Mountains are born from turmoil: enormous masses of land collide, clashing like armor over a molten core. During their tumultuous birth, these titans are barren.

No one can build a home upon the warring cliffs.

There is power in peace unlike any other, Peace Maker. Consider the tale of Chullabodhi, the conqueror of anger. Chullabodhi was an incarnation of the Bodhisatta, who, upon remembering his past lives, abandoned his brilliant scholarly career for an ascetic life. His wife followed suit. One day, while Chullabodhi mended his garb and his wife meditated under a tree, the king of the country arrived at their forest hermitage. Smitten with the chiseled beauty of the wife, the king fancied abducting her, yet held back, afraid that Chullabodhi might have supernatural powers.

So the king asked the hermit what he would do if someone were to carry his wife away.

“I would not release it,” Chullabodhi said, without looking up from his needle.

The king frowned. “Release what?”

“Anger,” the ascetic replied. “It is like a fire carried in the hands. Once thrown, it no longer belongs to the thrower. It burns whatever it touches — including the one who cast it.”

The king said nothing more. When he left the grove, the image stayed with him: fire leaving the hand, impossible to call back. It returned to him later, in the press of judgment and command. Each time anger rose, the image checked him. His rule continued — but with a hand that learned to hold its fire.

True courage does not always sound like ringing steel. Sometimes it lives in compassion and respectful silence. At the dawning of his path, a warrior seeks conflict to prove his worth. A mature warrior though reaches not for the hilt, but for a healing word. To preserve is infinitely more valuable than to win.

Peace Maker, bury your sword: Choosing peace does not mean capitulating. It means letting trees bear fruits rather than feeding them to flames of rage; to see conflict as a test of wisdom, a clash of wills. Remember: you have might, and you must use it to hold life in the palm of your hand, not to crush it. True power lies in the creation of space upon which peace can blossom.

text.en: Card text in English.

Humble Disciple

The clever know what they know, the wise—what they do not.

Have you lost the straightforward path, Humble Disciple? These lands are shifting and obscure. Now and then, the landscape alters fully between steps. Other times, forking paths confound the ability to choose. The rules that govern this perplexing landscape slip away from your untrained eye. But worry not—you’ve only just arrived! There is plenty of time to learn to navigate your new surroundings.

Seek wisdom in the unknown.

A wise man once said, “Life is the only true teacher.” Life alone does not learn from its students. Yet all people, even those who teach, learn from others. Mothers learn from their children, masters learn from their apprentices. There is no shame in learning, no need to conceal confusion behind brash confidence. To ask questions, observe, and explore will grant safe passage through obscurity.

The transience of youth is an illusion. A person has many youths—one for every new path they take. Time and time again, one is reborn into new forms: craftsman, lover, parent, friend. Each person and each skill is enigmatic. There is no skeleton key to life; wisdom in one aspect of life will not amend ignorance in another. Take pleasure in the fact that learning is a constant: it testifies to the inexhaustible richness of life. In order to move with the rhythm of discovery, you must only become curious.

Humble Disciple, let the unknown spark your curiosity: Seek out as many teachers as you can. You might find instruction where you least expect it. Return to memory for guidance: there is wisdom in both your and others’ mistakes. Discipline is important in learning, but so is pleasure. Crave knowledge and delight in finding it. Consider what might seal you off from learning. Are you afraid of failure? Remember that frustration is the precursor of wisdom.

Your mind is a garden, Humble Disciple. Accepting your ignorance creates fertile ground for new insights. New journeys, stalemates, and labyrinthine situations are seeds that blossom into knowledge.
